cards.name: [P1] Transcend Fund - Shanti Bergel
cards.description:
**Fund:** Transcend Fund
**Partner:** Shanti Bergel
**Email:** shanti@transcend.fund
**Fit Score:** 92/100
**Stage:** Seed to Series A
**Check Size:** $500K - $3M
**Tags:** gaming, AI, seed, series-a

**Outreach Angle:**
Transcend's back-to-back investments in Ruckus Games' $19M Series A and Live Aware Labs' AI-powered game dev platform show clear conviction in AI as the foundational layer for next-gen multiplayer experiences. NEXUS AI builds the infrastructure layer that makes dynamic, AI-powered game worlds scalable.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: shanti@transcend.fund
Subject: Ruckus Games + Live Aware Labs portfolio → AI infrastructure for dynamic worlds

Hi Shanti,

Transcend's back-to-back investments in Ruckus Games' $19M Series A and Live Aware Labs' AI-powered game dev platform caught my attention. You're clearly betting on AI as the foundational layer for next-gen multiplayer experiences.

I'm reaching out because NEXUS AI is building the infrastructure layer that makes dynamic, AI-powered game worlds actually scalable. While Live Aware is revolutionizing QA and development workflows, NEXUS handles the runtime architecture—enabling persistent AI agents, procedural narrative systems, and worlds that evolve based on player behavior without ballooning compute costs.

The founders come from [REDACTED - add relevant background]. We're seeing 10x efficiency gains for studios building persistent multiplayer environments, which feels aligned with where Transcend's portfolio is heading.

Worth a 15-minute conversation? I'd love to share what we're seeing from AAA studios already piloting the platform.

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
[Founder Name]
NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-02-28

cards.name: [P2] Konvoy Ventures - Jason Chapman
cards.description:
**Fund:** Konvoy Ventures
**Partner:** Jason Chapman
**Email:** jason@konvoy.vc
**Fit Score:** 90/100
**Stage:** Seed to Series A
**Check Size:** $500K - $2.5M
**Tags:** gaming, infrastructure, seed, series-a

**Outreach Angle:**
Congratulations on the Ready Player Me exit—that was a textbook Konvoy bet on infrastructure over content. Konvoy's thesis on gaming platforms and frontier tech aligns perfectly as the industry shifts from static worlds to dynamic, AI-generated experiences.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: jason@konvoy.vc
Subject: Post-Ready Player Me → infrastructure for the next gaming frontier

Hi Jason,

Congratulations on the Ready Player Me exit—that was a textbook Konvoy bet on infrastructure over content. Your thesis on gaming platforms and frontier tech has always resonated with me, especially as the industry shifts from static worlds to dynamic, AI-generated experiences.

NEXUS AI is building the infrastructure layer that enables exactly that transition. We're creating the runtime environment for persistent AI agents in multiplayer game worlds—think NPCs with memory, evolving world states, and procedural narrative systems that don't require massive AI compute budgets per player.

Given Konvoy's focus on infrastructure and your investments in Battle Road (planet-scale simulations) and metaverse-enabling tech, NEXUS feels like a natural fit for the portfolio. We're already working with [STUDIO NAME REDACTED] to reduce their AI runtime costs by 60% while enabling persistent world states they couldn't build before.

Would you have 15 minutes next week to discuss how AI infrastructure is becoming the new middleware layer for gaming?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
[Founder Name]
NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-02-28

cards.name: [P3] Hiro Capital - Luke Alvarez
cards.description:
**Fund:** Hiro Capital
**Partner:** Luke Alvarez
**Email:** luke@hiro.capital
**Fit Score:** 88/100
**Stage:** Series A to B
**Check Size:** $3M - $10M
**Tags:** gaming, spatial-AI, series-a, series-b

**Outreach Angle:**
Between FRVR's GenAI game creation platform and the recent Sidero Labs investment (plus Sir Nick Clegg joining for Spatial AI), Hiro Capital is positioning at the intersection of AI, infrastructure, and spatial computing. NEXUS handles the runtime infrastructure that makes Spatial AI viable at scale.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: luke@hiro.capital
Subject: FRVR + Sidero Labs → the missing runtime layer for Spatial AI

Hi Luke,

Between FRVR's GenAI game creation platform and your recent Sidero Labs investment (plus Sir Nick Clegg joining for Spatial AI), Hiro Capital is clearly positioning at the intersection of AI, infrastructure, and spatial computing. I think there's a critical piece of that stack you haven't seen yet.

NEXUS AI is building the runtime infrastructure that makes Spatial AI actually viable at scale. While FRVR empowers creators with AI tools and Sidero optimizes container orchestration, NEXUS handles the hard problem: running persistent AI agents, dynamic world states, and real-time inference in multiplayer environments without crushing unit economics.

Our platform is already enabling European game studios to ship AI-powered worlds that were previously cost-prohibitive. With Hiro III deploying capital into this exact thesis, I'd love to show you what we're building.

Do you have 15 minutes for a quick call? I think NEXUS fits squarely in the infrastructure gap you're targeting.

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
[Founder Name]
NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

cards.name: [P4] London Venture Partners - David Lau-Kee
cards.description:
**Fund:** London Venture Partners (LVP)
**Partner:** David Lau-Kee
**Email:** dlk@londonvp.com
**Fit Score:** 87/100
**Stage:** Seed
**Check Size:** $500K - $2M
**Tags:** gaming, seed, AI-infrastructure

**Outreach Angle:**
LVP's track record—early checks into Unity and Supercell defined the last decade of gaming. Recent Bitpart.AI investment ($3.3M seed) shows conviction that AI is fundamentally changing game worlds. NEXUS addresses the gap: infrastructure to run AI at scale in production multiplayer environments.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: dlk@londonvp.com
Subject: From Unity/Supercell to Bitpart.AI → the infrastructure thesis continues

Hi David,

LVP's track record speaks for itself—early checks into Unity and Supercell defined the last decade of gaming. Your recent Bitpart.AI investment ($3.3M seed) shows you're seeing what I'm seeing: AI isn't just changing content creation, it's fundamentally changing what's possible in game worlds.

But here's the gap no one's addressing yet: infrastructure. Bitpart enables smarter NPCs, but studios still struggle to run AI at scale in production multiplayer environments. NEXUS AI is building that runtime layer—enabling persistent AI agents, evolving world states, and procedural systems without the compute costs that kill margins.

Given your Criterion/RenderWare heritage, you understand better than most that every content revolution needs a corresponding infrastructure layer. We're already piloting with [STUDIO NAMES REDACTED] and seeing 10x efficiency gains on AI-powered features.

Worth a 15-minute conversation? I think NEXUS belongs in the same conversation as Bitpart for the next generation of gaming infrastructure.

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
[Founder Name]
NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

cards.name: [P5] F4 Fund - David Kaye
cards.description:
**Fund:** F4 Fund
**Partner:** David Kaye
**Email:** david@f4.fund
**Fit Score:** 85/100
**Stage:** Seed
**Check Size:** $250K - $1M
**Tags:** gaming, founder-first, seed, gaming-alumni

**Outreach Angle:**
F4 co-invested in Series' $28M round alongside a16z and BITKRAFT—smart bet on GenAI game development. But missing the infrastructure layer that makes AI-powered games viable in production. NEXUS is built by founders from gaming backgrounds, similar to F4's founder-first approach.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: david@f4.fund
Subject: Series co-investor here → another gaming founder building AI infrastructure

Hi David,

I saw F4 co-invested in Series' $28M round alongside a16z and BITKRAFT. Smart bet on GenAI game development. I'm reaching out because you're missing the other half of that opportunity—the infrastructure layer that makes AI-powered games actually viable in production.

NEXUS AI is built by founders who came from [GAMING BACKGROUND REDACTED], similar to the gaming veterans F4 backs. We experienced firsthand the pain of trying to ship AI-powered multiplayer worlds: inference costs that scale linearly with players, state management nightmares, and the architecture gaps that kill ambitious projects.

So we built the infrastructure layer we wished existed. NEXUS enables persistent AI agents, dynamic world evolution, and real-time inference at unit economics that actually work for live games. Studios are cutting AI runtime costs by 60%+ while shipping features they couldn't build before.

As a former founder yourself (Iron Realms, Meteor Games), you know the difference between demo AI and production AI. I'd love 15 minutes to show you what we're building for the next generation of gaming founders.

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
[Founder Name]
NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-02
